ayahs.arabic: ۞ وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَرْجُونَ لِقَآءَنَا لَوْلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْنَا ٱلْمَلَٰٓئِكَةُ أَوْ نَرَىٰ رَبَّنَا ۗ لَقَدِ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ فِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ وَعَتَوْ عُتُوًّۭا كَبِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: But those who do not believe that they are destined to meet Us are wont to say, “Why have no angels been sent down to us?” – or, “Why do we not see our Sustainer?” Indeed, they are far too proud of themselves, hav­ing rebelled [against God’s truth] with utter disdain!

ayahs.arabic: يَوْمَ يَرَوْنَ ٱلْمَلَٰٓئِكَةَ لَا بُشْرَىٰ يَوْمَئِذٍۢ لِّلْمُجْرِمِينَ وَيَقُولُونَ حِجْرًۭا مَّحْجُورًۭا
ayahs.translation: [Yet] on that Day - the Day on which they shall see the angels there will be no glad tiding for those who were lost in sin; and they will exclaim, “By a forbidding ban [are we from God’s grace debarred]!” –

ayahs.arabic: وَقَدِمْنَآ إِلَىٰ مَا عَمِلُوا۟ مِنْ عَمَلٍۢ فَجَعَلْنَٰهُ هَبَآءًۭ مَّنثُورًا
ayahs.translation: for We shall have turned towards all the [supposedly good] deeds they ever wrought, and shall have transformed them into scattered dust –

ayahs.arabic: أَصْحَٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ يَوْمَئِذٍ خَيْرٌۭ مُّسْتَقَرًّۭا وَأَحْسَنُ مَقِيلًۭا
ayahs.translation: [whereas] on that same Day those who are destined for paradise will be graced with the best of abodes and the fairest place of repose.

ayahs.arabic: وَيَوْمَ تَشَقَّقُ ٱلسَّمَآءُ بِٱلْغَمَٰمِ وَنُزِّلَ ٱلْمَلَٰٓئِكَةُ تَنزِيلًا
ayahs.translation: And on the Day on which the skies, together with the clouds, shall burst asunder, and the angels are made to descend in a mighty descent –

ayahs.arabic: ٱلْمُلْكُ يَوْمَئِذٍ ٱلْحَقُّ لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ ۚ وَكَانَ يَوْمًا عَلَى ٱلْكَٰفِرِينَ عَسِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: on that Day [it will become obvious to all that] true sover­eignty belongs to the Most Gracious [alone]: hence, a Day of distress will it be for all who deny the truth,

ayahs.arabic: وَيَوْمَ يَعَضُّ ٱلظَّالِمُ عَلَىٰ يَدَيْهِ يَقُولُ يَٰلَيْتَنِى ٱتَّخَذْتُ مَعَ ٱلرَّسُولِ سَبِيلًۭا
ayahs.translation: and a Day on which the evildoer will bite his hands [in despair], exclaiming: “Oh, would that I had followed the path shown to me by the apostle!

ayahs.arabic: يَٰوَيْلَتَىٰ لَيْتَنِى لَمْ أَتَّخِذْ فُلَانًا خَلِيلًۭا
ayahs.translation: Oh, woe is me! Would that I had not taken so-and-so for a friend!

ayahs.arabic: لَّقَدْ أَضَلَّنِى عَنِ ٱلذِّكْرِ بَعْدَ إِذْ جَآءَنِى ۗ وَكَانَ ٱلشَّيْطَٰنُ لِلْإِنسَٰنِ خَذُولًۭا
ayahs.translation: Indeed, he led me astray from the remembrance [of God] after it had come unto me!” For [thus it is:] Satan is ever a betrayer of man.

ayahs.arabic: وَقَالَ ٱلرَّسُولُ يَٰرَبِّ إِنَّ قَوْمِى ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ هَٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانَ مَهْجُورًۭا
ayahs.translation: AND [on that Day] the Apostle will say: “O my Sustainer! Behold, [some of] my people have come to regard this Qur’an as something [that ought to be] discarded!”

ayahs.arabic: وَكَذَٰلِكَ جَعَلْنَا لِكُلِّ نَبِىٍّ عَدُوًّۭا مِّنَ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ ۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِرَبِّكَ هَادِيًۭا وَنَصِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: For so it is that against every prophet We have set up enemies from among those who are lost in sin: yet none can guide and give succour as thy Sustainer does!

ayahs.arabic: وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَوْلَا نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ ٱلْقُرْءَانُ جُمْلَةًۭ وَٰحِدَةًۭ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ لِنُثَبِّتَ بِهِۦ فُؤَادَكَ ۖ وَرَتَّلْنَٰهُ تَرْتِيلًۭا
ayahs.translation: Now they who are bent on denying the truth are wont to ask. “Why has not the Qur’an been bestowed on him from on high in one single re­velation?” [it has been revealed] in this manner so that We might strengthen thy heart thereby - for We have so arranged its component parts that they form one consistent whole -

ayahs.arabic: وَلَا يَأْتُونَكَ بِمَثَلٍ إِلَّا جِئْنَٰكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَأَحْسَنَ تَفْسِيرًا
ayahs.translation: and [that] they [who deny the truth] might never taunt thee with any deceptive half-truth without Our conveying to thee the [full] truth and [providing thee] with the best explanation.

ayahs.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يُحْشَرُونَ عَلَىٰ وُجُوهِهِمْ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ أُو۟لَٰٓئِكَ شَرٌّۭ مَّكَانًۭا وَأَضَلُّ سَبِيلًۭا
ayahs.translation: [And so, tell those who are bent on denying the truth that] they who shall be gathered unto hell upon their faces – it is they who [in the life to come] will be worst in station and still farther astray from the path [of truth]!

ayahs.arabic: وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَٰبَ وَجَعَلْنَا مَعَهُۥٓ أَخَاهُ هَٰرُونَ وَزِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: AND, INDEED, [long before Muhammad] We vouchsafed revelation unto Moses, and appointed his brother Aaron to help him to bear his burden;

ayahs.arabic: فَقُلْنَا ٱذْهَبَآ إِلَى ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَا فَدَمَّرْنَٰهُمْ تَدْمِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: and We said, “Go you both unto the people who have given the lie to Our messages!” - and thereupon We broke those [sinners] to smithereens.

words.translation: ফলে আমরা ধ্বংস করে দিয়েছি তাদেরকে

ayahs.arabic: وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍۢ لَّمَّا كَذَّبُوا۟ ٱلرُّسُلَ أَغْرَقْنَٰهُمْ وَجَعَلْنَٰهُمْ لِلنَّاسِ ءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَأَعْتَدْنَا لِلظَّٰلِمِينَ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًۭا
ayahs.translation: And [think of] the people of Noah: when they gave the lie to [one of] the apostles, We caused them to drown, and made them a symbol for all mankind: for, grievous suffering have We readied for all who [knowingly] do wrong!

ayahs.arabic: وَعَادًۭا وَثَمُودَا۟ وَأَصْحَٰبَ ٱلرَّسِّ وَقُرُونًۢا بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ كَثِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: And [remember how We punished the tribes of] Ad and Thamud and the people of Ar-Rass, and many generations [of sinners] in-between:

ayahs.arabic: وَكُلًّۭا ضَرَبْنَا لَهُ ٱلْأَمْثَٰلَ ۖ وَكُلًّۭا تَبَّرْنَا تَتْبِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: and unto each of them did We proffer lessons and each of them did We destroy with utter destruction.

ayahs.arabic: وَلَقَدْ أَتَوْا۟ عَلَى ٱلْقَرْيَةِ ٱلَّتِىٓ أُمْطِرَتْ مَطَرَ ٱلسَّوْءِ ۚ أَفَلَمْ يَكُونُوا۟ يَرَوْنَهَا ۚ بَلْ كَانُوا۟ لَا يَرْجُونَ نُشُورًۭا
ayahs.translation: And they [who now deny Our messages] must surely have come across that town which was rained upon by a rain of evil: have they, then, never beheld it [with their minds eye]? But nay, they would not believe in resurrection!

ayahs.arabic: وَإِذَا رَأَوْكَ إِن يَتَّخِذُونَكَ إِلَّا هُزُوًا أَهَٰذَا ٱلَّذِى بَعَثَ ٱللَّهُ رَسُولًا
ayahs.translation: Hence, whenever they consider thee, [O Muhammad,] they but make thee a target of their mockery, [saying:] “Is this the one whom God has sent as an apostle?

ayahs.arabic: إِن كَادَ لَيُضِلُّنَا عَنْ ءَالِهَتِنَا لَوْلَآ أَن صَبَرْنَا عَلَيْهَا ۚ وَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ حِينَ يَرَوْنَ ٱلْعَذَابَ مَنْ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا
ayahs.translation: Indeed, he would well-nigh have led us astray from our deities, had we not been [so] steadfastly attached to them!” But in time, when they see the suffering [that awaits them], they will come to know who it was that went farthest astray from the path [of truth]!

ayahs.arabic: أَرَءَيْتَ مَنِ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَٰهَهُۥ هَوَىٰهُ أَفَأَنتَ تَكُونُ عَلَيْهِ وَكِيلًا
ayahs.translation: Hast thou ever considered [the kind of man] who makes his own desires his deity? Couldst thou, then, [O Prophet,] be held responsible for him?

ayahs.arabic: أَمْ تَحْسَبُ أَنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ يَسْمَعُونَ أَوْ يَعْقِلُونَ ۚ إِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا كَٱلْأَنْعَٰمِ ۖ بَلْ هُمْ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا
ayahs.translation: Or dost thou think that most of them listen [to thy message] and use their reason? Nay, they are but like cattle - nay, they are even less conscious of the right way!

ayahs.arabic: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَيْفَ مَدَّ ٱلظِّلَّ وَلَوْ شَآءَ لَجَعَلَهُۥ سَاكِنًۭا ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَا ٱلشَّمْسَ عَلَيْهِ دَلِيلًۭا
ayahs.translation: Art thou not aware of thy Sustainer [through His works]? - how He causes the shadow to lengthen [towards the night] when, had He so willed, He could indeed have made it stand still: but then, We have made the sun its guide;

ayahs.arabic: ثُمَّ قَبَضْنَٰهُ إِلَيْنَا قَبْضًۭا يَسِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: and then, [after having caused it to lengthen,] We draw it in towards Our­selves with a gradual drawing-in.

ayahs.arabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلَّيْلَ لِبَاسًۭا وَٱلنَّوْمَ سُبَاتًۭا وَجَعَلَ ٱلنَّهَارَ نُشُورًۭا
ayahs.translation: And He it is who makes the night a garment for you, and [your] sleep a rest, and causes every [new] day to be a resurrection.

ayahs.arabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَرْسَلَ ٱلرِّيَٰحَ بُشْرًۢا بَيْنَ يَدَىْ رَحْمَتِهِۦ ۚ وَأَنزَلْنَا مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءًۭ طَهُورًۭا
ayahs.translation: And He it is who sends forth the winds as a glad tiding of His coming grace; and [thus, too,] We cause pure water to descend from the skies,

ayahs.arabic: لِّنُحْۦِىَ بِهِۦ بَلْدَةًۭ مَّيْتًۭا وَنُسْقِيَهُۥ مِمَّا خَلَقْنَآ أَنْعَٰمًۭا وَأَنَاسِىَّ كَثِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: so that We may bring dead land to life thereby, and give to drink thereof to many [beings] of Our creation, beasts as well as humans.

ayahs.arabic: وَلَقَدْ صَرَّفْنَٰهُ بَيْنَهُمْ لِيَذَّكَّرُوا۟ فَأَبَىٰٓ أَكْثَرُ ٱلنَّاسِ إِلَّا كُفُورًۭا
ayahs.translation: And, indeed, many times have We repeated [all] this unto men, so that they might take it to heart: but most men refuse to be aught but ingrate.

ayahs.arabic: وَلَوْ شِئْنَا لَبَعَثْنَا فِى كُلِّ قَرْيَةٍۢ نَّذِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: Now had We so willed. We could have [con­tinued as before and] raised up a [separate] warner in every single community:

ayahs.arabic: فَلَا تُطِعِ ٱلْكَٰفِرِينَ وَجَٰهِدْهُم بِهِۦ جِهَادًۭا كَبِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: hence, do not defer to [the likes and dislikes of] those who deny the truth, but strive hard against them, by means of this [divine writ], with utmost striving.

ayahs.arabic: ۞ وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى مَرَجَ ٱلْبَحْرَيْنِ هَٰذَا عَذْبٌۭ فُرَاتٌۭ وَهَٰذَا مِلْحٌ أُجَاجٌۭ وَجَعَلَ بَيْنَهُمَا بَرْزَخًۭا وَحِجْرًۭا مَّحْجُورًۭا
ayahs.translation: AND HE it is who has given freedom of movement to the two great bodies of water – the one sweet and thirst-allaying, and the other salty and bitter - and yet has wrought between them a barrier and a forbidding ban.

ayahs.arabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ مِنَ ٱلْمَآءِ بَشَرًۭا فَجَعَلَهُۥ نَسَبًۭا وَصِهْرًۭا ۗ وَكَانَ رَبُّكَ قَدِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: And He it is who out of this [very] water has created man, and has endowed him with [the con­sciousness of] descent and marriage-tie: for thy Sustainer is ever infinite in His power.

ayahs.arabic: وَيَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنفَعُهُمْ وَلَا يَضُرُّهُمْ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱلْكَافِرُ عَلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ ظَهِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: And yet, some people worship, instead of God, things that can neither benefit them nor harm them: thus, he who denies the truth does indeed turn his back on his Sustainer!

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَٰكَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرًۭا وَنَذِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: Yet [withal, O Prophet,] We have sent thee only as a herald of glad tidings and a warner.

ayahs.arabic: قُلْ مَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ إِلَّا مَن شَآءَ أَن يَتَّخِذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ سَبِيلًۭا
ayahs.translation: Say: “For this, no reward do I ask of you [- no reward] other than that he who so wills may unto his Sustainer find a way!”

ayahs.arabic: وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱلْحَىِّ ٱلَّذِى لَا يَمُوتُ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِهِۦ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِهِۦ بِذُنُوبِ عِبَادِهِۦ خَبِيرًا
ayahs.translation: Hence, place thy trust in the Living One who dies not, and extol His limitless glory and praise: for none is as aware as His creatures’ sins as He –

ayahs.arabic: ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍۢ ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ ۚ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنُ فَسْـَٔلْ بِهِۦ خَبِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: He who has created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in six aeons, and is established on the throne of His almightiness: the Most Gracious! Ask, then, about Him, [the] One who is [truly] aware.

ayahs.arabic: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ ٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ قَالُوا۟ وَمَا ٱلرَّحْمَٰنُ أَنَسْجُدُ لِمَا تَأْمُرُنَا وَزَادَهُمْ نُفُورًۭا ۩
ayahs.translation: Yet when they [who are bent on denying the truth! are told, “Prostrate yourselves before the Most Gracious.” they are wont to ask, “And [who and] what is the Most Gracious? Are we to prostrate ourselves before whatever thou biddest us [to worship]?” - and so [thy call] but increases their aversion,

ayahs.arabic: تَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ بُرُوجًۭا وَجَعَلَ فِيهَا سِرَٰجًۭا وَقَمَرًۭا مُّنِيرًۭا
ayahs.translation: HALLOWED is He who has set up in the skies great constellations, and has placed among them a [radiant] lamp and a light-giving moon.

ayahs.arabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ ٱلَّيْلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ خِلْفَةًۭ لِّمَنْ أَرَادَ أَن يَذَّكَّرَ أَوْ أَرَادَ شُكُورًۭا
ayahs.translation: And He it is who causes the night and the day to succeed one another, [revealing Himself in His works] unto him who has the will to take thought - that is has the will to be grateful.

ayahs.arabic: وَعِبَادُ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلَّذِينَ يَمْشُونَ عَلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ هَوْنًۭا وَإِذَا خَاطَبَهُمُ ٱلْجَٰهِلُونَ قَالُوا۟ سَلَٰمًۭا
ayahs.translation: For, [true] servants of the Most Gracious are [only] they who walk gently on earth, and who, whenever the foolish address them, reply with [words of] peace;

ayahs.arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ يَبِيتُونَ لِرَبِّهِمْ سُجَّدًۭا وَقِيَٰمًۭا
ayahs.translation: and who remember their Sustainer far into the night, prostrating themselves and standing;

ayahs.arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا ٱصْرِفْ عَنَّا عَذَابَ جَهَنَّمَ ۖ إِنَّ عَذَابَهَا كَانَ غَرَامًا
ayahs.translation: and who pray: “O our Sustainer, avert from us the suffering of hell - for, verily, the suffering caused by it is bound to be a torment dire:

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّهَا سَآءَتْ مُسْتَقَرًّۭا وَمُقَامًۭا
ayahs.translation: verily, how evil an abode and a station!” -;

ayahs.arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ إِذَآ أَنفَقُوا۟ لَمْ يُسْرِفُوا۟ وَلَمْ يَقْتُرُوا۟ وَكَانَ بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ قَوَامًۭا
ayahs.translation: and who, whenever they spend on others, are neither wasteful nor niggardly but [remember that] there is always a just mean between those [two extremes];

ayahs.arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَدْعُونَ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا ءَاخَرَ وَلَا يَقْتُلُونَ ٱلنَّفْسَ ٱلَّتِى حَرَّمَ ٱللَّهُ إِلَّا بِٱلْحَقِّ وَلَا يَزْنُونَ ۚ وَمَن يَفْعَلْ ذَٰلِكَ يَلْقَ أَثَامًۭا
ayahs.translation: and who never invoke any [imaginary] deity side by side with God, and do not take any human beings life - [the life] which God has willed to be sacred - otherwise than in [the pursuit of] justice, and do not commit adultery. And [know that] he who commits aught thereof shall [not only] meet with a full requital

ayahs.arabic: يُضَٰعَفْ لَهُ ٱلْعَذَابُ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَٰمَةِ وَيَخْلُدْ فِيهِۦ مُهَانًا
ayahs.translation: [but] shall have his suffering doubled on Resurrection Day: for on that [Day] he shall abide in ignominy.

ayahs.arabic: إِلَّا مَن تَابَ وَءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ عَمَلًۭا صَٰلِحًۭا فَأُو۟لَٰٓئِكَ يُبَدِّلُ ٱللَّهُ سَيِّـَٔاتِهِمْ حَسَنَٰتٍۢ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًۭا رَّحِيمًۭا
ayahs.translation: Excepted, however, shall be they who repent and attain to faith and do righteous deeds: for it is they whose [erstwhile] bad deeds God will transform into good ones - seeing that God is indeed much-forgiving, a dispenser of grace,

ayahs.arabic: وَمَن تَابَ وَعَمِلَ صَٰلِحًۭا فَإِنَّهُۥ يَتُوبُ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ مَتَابًۭا
ayahs.translation: and seeing that he who repents and [thenceforth] does what is right has truly turned unto God by [this very act of] repent­ance.

ayahs.arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَشْهَدُونَ ٱلزُّورَ وَإِذَا مَرُّوا۟ بِٱللَّغْوِ مَرُّوا۟ كِرَامًۭا
ayahs.translation: And [know that true servants of God are only] those who never bear witness to what is false, and [who], whenever they pass by [people engaged in] frivolity, pass on with dignity;

ayahs.arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِّرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ لَمْ يَخِرُّوا۟ عَلَيْهَا صُمًّۭا وَعُمْيَانًۭا
ayahs.translation: and who, whenever they are reminded of their Sustainer’s messages, do not throw themselves upon them [as if] deaf and blind;

ayahs.arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا هَبْ لَنَا مِنْ أَزْوَٰجِنَا وَذُرِّيَّٰتِنَا قُرَّةَ أَعْيُنٍۢ وَٱجْعَلْنَا لِلْمُتَّقِينَ إِمَامًا
ayahs.translation: and who pray “O our Sustainer! Grant that our spouses and our offspring be a joy to our eyes, and cause us to be foremost among those who are conscious of Thee!”

ayahs.arabic: أُو۟لَٰٓئِكَ يُجْزَوْنَ ٱلْغُرْفَةَ بِمَا صَبَرُوا۟ وَيُلَقَّوْنَ فِيهَا تَحِيَّةًۭ وَسَلَٰمًا
ayahs.translation: [Such as] these will be rewarded for all their patient endurance [in life] with a high station [in paradise], and will be met therein with a greeting of welcome and peace,

ayahs.arabic: خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ حَسُنَتْ مُسْتَقَرًّۭا وَمُقَامًۭا
ayahs.translation: therein to abide: [and] how goodly an abode and [how high] a station!

ayahs.arabic: قُلْ مَا يَعْبَؤُا۟ بِكُمْ رَبِّى لَوْلَا دُعَآؤُكُمْ ۖ فَقَدْ كَذَّبْتُمْ فَسَوْفَ يَكُونُ لِزَامًۢا
ayahs.translation: SAY [unto those who believe]: “No weight or value would my Sustainer attach to you were it not for your faith [in Him]!” And say unto those who deny the truth:] “You have indeed given the lie [to God’s message], and in time this [sin] will cleave unto you!”

ayahs.arabic: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ طسٓمٓ
ayahs.translation: Ta. Sin, Mim.

ayahs.arabic: تِلْكَ ءَايَٰتُ ٱلْكِتَٰبِ ٱلْمُبِينِ
ayahs.translation: THESE ARE MESSAGES of the divine writ, clear in itself and clearly showing the truth!

ayahs.arabic: لَعَلَّكَ بَٰخِعٌۭ نَّفْسَكَ أَلَّا يَكُونُوا۟ مُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: Wouldst thou, perhaps, torment thyself to death [with grief] because they [who live around thee] refuse to believe [in it]?

ayahs.arabic: إِن نَّشَأْ نُنَزِّلْ عَلَيْهِم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ ءَايَةًۭ فَظَلَّتْ أَعْنَٰقُهُمْ لَهَا خَٰضِعِينَ
ayahs.translation: Had We so willed, We could have sent down unto them a message from the skies, so that their necks would [be forced to] bow down before it in humility.

ayahs.arabic: وَمَا يَأْتِيهِم مِّن ذِكْرٍۢ مِّنَ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ مُحْدَثٍ إِلَّا كَانُوا۟ عَنْهُ مُعْرِضِينَ
ayahs.translation: [But We have not willed it:] and so, whenever there comes unto them any fresh reminder from the Most Gracious, they [who are blind of heart] always - turn their backs upon it:

ayahs.arabic: فَقَدْ كَذَّبُوا۟ فَسَيَأْتِيهِمْ أَنۢبَٰٓؤُا۟ مَا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ
ayahs.translation: thus, indeed, have they given the lie (to this message as well). But [in time] they will come to understand what it was that they were wont to deride!

ayahs.arabic: أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ إِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ كَمْ أَنۢبَتْنَا فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ زَوْجٍۢ كَرِيمٍ
ayahs.translation: Have they, then, never considered the earth - how much of every noble kind [of life] We have caused to grow thereon?

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: In this, behold, there is a message [unto men], even though most of them will not believe [in it].

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
ayahs.translation: But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!

ayahs.arabic: وَإِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبُّكَ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنِ ٱئْتِ ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.translation: HENCE, [remember how it was] when thy Sustainer summoned Moses: “Go unto those evil doing people.

ayahs.arabic: قَوْمَ فِرْعَوْنَ ۚ أَلَا يَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.translation: the people of Pharaoh, who refuse to be con­scious of Me!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ أَن يُكَذِّبُونِ
ayahs.translation: He answered: “O my Sustainer! Behold, I fear that they will give me the lie,

ayahs.arabic: وَيَضِيقُ صَدْرِى وَلَا يَنطَلِقُ لِسَانِى فَأَرْسِلْ إِلَىٰ هَٰرُونَ
ayahs.translation: and then my breast will be straitened and my tongue will not be free: send, then, [this Thy command] to Aaron.

ayahs.arabic: وَلَهُمْ عَلَىَّ ذَنۢبٌۭ فَأَخَافُ أَن يَقْتُلُونِ
ayahs.translation: Moreover, they keep a grave charge [pending] against me, and I fear that they will slay me.”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ كَلَّا ۖ فَٱذْهَبَا بِـَٔايَٰتِنَآ ۖ إِنَّا مَعَكُم مُّسْتَمِعُونَ
ayahs.translation: Said He: “Not so, indeed! Go forth, then, both of you, with Our messages: verify, We shall be with you, listening [to your call]!

ayahs.arabic: فَأْتِيَا فِرْعَوْنَ فَقُولَآ إِنَّا رَسُولُ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: And go, both of you, unto Pharaoh and say, ‘Behold, we bear a message from the Sustainer of all the worlds:

ayahs.arabic: أَنْ أَرْسِلْ مَعَنَا بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ
ayahs.translation: Let the children of Israel go with us!’”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ أَلَمْ نُرَبِّكَ فِينَا وَلِيدًۭا وَلَبِثْتَ فِينَا مِنْ عُمُرِكَ سِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: [But when Moses had delivered his message, Pharaoh] said: “Did we not bring thee up among us when thou wert a child? And didst thou not spend among us years of thy [later] life?

ayahs.arabic: وَفَعَلْتَ فَعْلَتَكَ ٱلَّتِى فَعَلْتَ وَأَنتَ مِنَ ٱلْكَٰفِرِينَ
ayahs.translation: And yet thou didst commit that [heinous] deed of thine, and [hast thus shown that] thou art one of the ingrate!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ فَعَلْتُهَآ إِذًۭا وَأَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلضَّآلِّينَ
ayahs.translation: Replied [Moses]: “I committed it while I was still going astray;

ayahs.arabic: فَفَرَرْتُ مِنكُمْ لَمَّا خِفْتُكُمْ فَوَهَبَ لِى رَبِّى حُكْمًۭا وَجَعَلَنِى مِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ
ayahs.translation: and I fled from you because I feared you. But [since] then my Sustainer has endowed me with the ability to judge [between right and wrong], and has made me one of [His] message-­bearers.

ayahs.arabic: وَتِلْكَ نِعْمَةٌۭ تَمُنُّهَا عَلَىَّ أَنْ عَبَّدتَّ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ
ayahs.translation: And [as for] that favour of which thou so tauntingly remindest me - [was it not] due to thy having enslaved the children of Israel?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ وَمَا رَبُّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: Said Pharaoh: “And what [and who] is that ‘Sustainer of all the worlds’?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَآ ۖ إِن كُنتُم مُّوقِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: [Moses] answered: “[He is] the Sustainer of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them: if you would but [allow yourselves to] be convinced!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ لِمَنْ حَوْلَهُۥٓ أَلَا تَسْتَمِعُونَ
ayahs.translation: Said [Pharaoh] unto those around him: “Did you hear [what he said]?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ رَبُّكُمْ وَرَبُّ ءَابَآئِكُمُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
ayahs.translation: [And Moses] continued: “[He is] your Sus­tainer, [too,] as well as the Sustainer of your fore­fathers of old!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ إِنَّ رَسُولَكُمُ ٱلَّذِىٓ أُرْسِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ لَمَجْنُونٌۭ
ayahs.translation: [Pharaoh] exclaimed: “Behold, [this] your ‘apostle’ who [claims that he] has been sent unto you is mad indeed!

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ رَبُّ ٱلْمَشْرِقِ وَٱلْمَغْرِبِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَآ ۖ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ
ayahs.translation: [But Moses] went on: “[He of whom I speak is] the Sustainer of the east and the west and of all that is between the two”- as you would know] if you would but use your reason!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ لَئِنِ ٱتَّخَذْتَ إِلَٰهًا غَيْرِى لَأَجْعَلَنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلْمَسْجُونِينَ
ayahs.translation: Said [Pharaoh]: “Indeed, if thou choose to worship any deity other than me, I shall most cer­tainly throw thee into prison!

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ أَوَلَوْ جِئْتُكَ بِشَىْءٍۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
ayahs.translation: Said he: “Even if I should bring about before thee something that clearly shows the truth?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ فَأْتِ بِهِۦٓ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّٰدِقِينَ
ayahs.translation: [Pharaoh] answered: “Produce it, then, if thou art a man of truth!”

ayahs.arabic: فَأَلْقَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِىَ ثُعْبَانٌۭ مُّبِينٌۭ
ayahs.translation: Thereupon [Moses] threw down his staff - and lo! it was a serpent, plainly visible;

ayahs.arabic: وَنَزَعَ يَدَهُۥ فَإِذَا هِىَ بَيْضَآءُ لِلنَّٰظِرِينَ
ayahs.translation: and he drew forth his hand - and lo! it appeared [shining] white to the beholders.

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ لِلْمَلَإِ حَوْلَهُۥٓ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَسَٰحِرٌ عَلِيمٌۭ
ayahs.translation: Said [pharaoh] unto the great ones around him: “Verily, this is indeed a sorcerer of great knowledge

ayahs.arabic: يُرِيدُ أَن يُخْرِجَكُم مِّنْ أَرْضِكُم بِسِحْرِهِۦ فَمَاذَا تَأْمُرُونَ
ayahs.translation: who wants to drive you out of your land by his sorcery. What, then, do you advise?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوٓا۟ أَرْجِهْ وَأَخَاهُ وَٱبْعَثْ فِى ٱلْمَدَآئِنِ حَٰشِرِينَ
ayahs.translation: They answered: “Let him and his brother wait a while, and send unto all cities heralds

ayahs.arabic: يَأْتُوكَ بِكُلِّ سَحَّارٍ عَلِيمٍۢ
ayahs.translation: who shall assemble before thee all sorcerers of great knowl­edge”

ayahs.arabic: فَجُمِعَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ لِمِيقَٰتِ يَوْمٍۢ مَّعْلُومٍۢ
ayahs.translation: And so the sorcerers were assembled at a set time on a certain day,

ayahs.arabic: وَقِيلَ لِلنَّاسِ هَلْ أَنتُم مُّجْتَمِعُونَ
ayahs.translation: and the people were asked: Are you all present,

ayahs.arabic: لَعَلَّنَا نَتَّبِعُ ٱلسَّحَرَةَ إِن كَانُوا۟ هُمُ ٱلْغَٰلِبِينَ
ayahs.translation: so that we might follow [in the footsteps of] the sorcerers if it is they who prevail?”

ayahs.arabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ قَالُوا۟ لِفِرْعَوْنَ أَئِنَّ لَنَا لَأَجْرًا إِن كُنَّا نَحْنُ ٱلْغَٰلِبِينَ
ayahs.translation: Now when the sorcerers came, they said unto Pharaoh: “Verily, we ought to have a great reward if it is we who prevail.

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ نَعَمْ وَإِنَّكُمْ إِذًۭا لَّمِنَ ٱلْمُقَرَّبِينَ
ayahs.translation: Answered he: “Yea - and, verily, in that case you shall be among those who are near unto me.

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰٓ أَلْقُوا۟ مَآ أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ
ayahs.translation: [And] Moses said unto them: “Throw whatever you are going to throw!”

ayahs.arabic: فَأَلْقَوْا۟ حِبَالَهُمْ وَعِصِيَّهُمْ وَقَالُوا۟ بِعِزَّةِ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّا لَنَحْنُ ٱلْغَٰلِبُونَ
ayahs.translation: Thereupon they threw their [magic ropes and their staffs, and said: “By Pharaoh’s might, behold, it is we indeed who have prevailed!”

ayahs.arabic: فَأَلْقَىٰ مُوسَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِىَ تَلْقَفُ مَا يَأْفِكُونَ
ayahs.translation: [But] then Moses threw his staff - and lo! it swallowed up all their deceptions.

ayahs.arabic: فَأُلْقِىَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ سَٰجِدِينَ
ayahs.translation: And down fell the sorcerers, prostrating them­selves in adoration,

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: [and] exclaimed: “We have come to believe in the Sustainer of all the worlds,

ayahs.arabic: رَبِّ مُوسَىٰ وَهَٰرُونَ
ayahs.translation: the Sustainer of Moses and Aaron!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ ءَامَنتُمْ لَهُۥ قَبْلَ أَنْ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكَبِيرُكُمُ ٱلَّذِى عَلَّمَكُمُ ٱلسِّحْرَ فَلَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ ۚ لَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيْدِيَكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُم مِّنْ خِلَٰفٍۢ وَلَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ
ayahs.translation: Said [Pharaoh]: “Have you come to believe in him ere I have given you permission? Verily, he must be your master who has taught you magic! But in time you shall come to know [my revenge]: most certainly shall I cut off your hands and your feet in great numbers, because of [your] perverseness, and shall most certainly crucify you in great numbers, all together!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ لَا ضَيْرَ ۖ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا مُنقَلِبُونَ
ayahs.translation: They answered: “No harm [canst thou do to us]: verily, unto our Sustainer do we turn!

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّا نَطْمَعُ أَن يَغْفِرَ لَنَا رَبُّنَا خَطَٰيَٰنَآ أَن كُنَّآ أَوَّلَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: Behold, we [but] ardently desire that our Sustainer forgive us our faults in return for our having been foremost among the believers!”

ayahs.arabic: ۞ وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنْ أَسْرِ بِعِبَادِىٓ إِنَّكُم مُّتَّبَعُونَ
ayahs.translation: AND [there came a time when] We inspired Moses thus: “Go forth with My servants by night: for, behold, you will he pursued!”

ayahs.arabic: فَأَرْسَلَ فِرْعَوْنُ فِى ٱلْمَدَآئِنِ حَٰشِرِينَ
ayahs.translation: And Pharaoh sent heralds unto all cities,

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ هَٰٓؤُلَآءِ لَشِرْذِمَةٌۭ قَلِيلُونَ
ayahs.translation: [bidding them to call out his troops and to proclaim:] “Behold, these [children of Israel] are but a contemptible band;

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنَّهُمْ لَنَا لَغَآئِظُونَ
ayahs.translation: but they are indeed filled with hatred of us

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنَّا لَجَمِيعٌ حَٰذِرُونَ
ayahs.translation: seeing that we are, verily, a nation united, fully prepared against danger –

ayahs.arabic: فَأَخْرَجْنَٰهُم مِّن جَنَّٰتٍۢ وَعُيُونٍۢ
ayahs.translation: and so we have [rightly] driven them out of [their] gardens and springs,

ayahs.arabic: وَكُنُوزٍۢ وَمَقَامٍۢ كَرِيمٍۢ
ayahs.translation: and [deprived them of their erstwhile] station of honour!”

ayahs.arabic: كَذَٰلِكَ وَأَوْرَثْنَٰهَا بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ
ayahs.translation: Thus it was: but [in the course of time] We were to bestow all these [things] as a heritage on the children of Israel.

ayahs.arabic: فَأَتْبَعُوهُم مُّشْرِقِينَ
ayahs.translation: And so [the Egyptians] caught up with them at sunrise;

ayahs.arabic: فَلَمَّا تَرَٰٓءَا ٱلْجَمْعَانِ قَالَ أَصْحَٰبُ مُوسَىٰٓ إِنَّا لَمُدْرَكُونَ
ayahs.translation: and as soon as the two hosts came in sight of one another, the followers of Moses exclaimed: “Behold, we shall certainly be overtaken [and defeated]!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ كَلَّآ ۖ إِنَّ مَعِىَ رَبِّى سَيَهْدِينِ
ayahs.translation: He replied: Nay indeed! My Sustainer is with me, [and] He will guide me!”

ayahs.arabic: فَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنِ ٱضْرِب بِّعَصَاكَ ٱلْبَحْرَ ۖ فَٱنفَلَقَ فَكَانَ كُلُّ فِرْقٍۢ كَٱلطَّوْدِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ
ayahs.translation: Thereupon We inspired Moses thus: Strike the sea with thy staff!”- whereupon it parted, and each part appeared like a mountain vast.

ayahs.arabic: وَأَزْلَفْنَا ثَمَّ ٱلْءَاخَرِينَ
ayahs.translation: And We caused the pursuers to draw near unto that place:

ayahs.arabic: وَأَنجَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ وَمَن مَّعَهُۥٓ أَجْمَعِينَ
ayahs.translation: and We saved Moses and all who were with him,

ayahs.arabic: ثُمَّ أَغْرَقْنَا ٱلْءَاخَرِينَ
ayahs.translation: and then We caused the others to drown.

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: In this [story] behold, there is a message [unto all men], even though most of them will not believe [in it].

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
ayahs.translation: And yet, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!

ayahs.arabic: وَٱتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ
ayahs.translation: And convey unto them the story of Abraham –

ayahs.arabic: إِذْ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوْمِهِۦ مَا تَعْبُدُونَ
ayahs.translation: [how it was] when he asked his father and his people, “What is it that you worship?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ نَعْبُدُ أَصْنَامًۭا فَنَظَلُّ لَهَا عَٰكِفِينَ
ayahs.translation: They answered: We worship idols, and we remain ever devoted to them.”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ هَلْ يَسْمَعُونَكُمْ إِذْ تَدْعُونَ
ayahs.translation: Said he: “Do [you really think that] they hear you when you invoke them,

ayahs.arabic: أَوْ يَنفَعُونَكُمْ أَوْ يَضُرُّونَ
ayahs.translation: or benefit you or do you harm?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ بَلْ وَجَدْنَآ ءَابَآءَنَا كَذَٰلِكَ يَفْعَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: They exclaimed: But we found our fore­fathers doing the same!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ أَفَرَءَيْتُم مَّا كُنتُمْ تَعْبُدُونَ
ayahs.translation: Said [Abraham]: “Have you, then, ever con­sidered what it is that you have been worshipping

ayahs.arabic: أَنتُمْ وَءَابَآؤُكُمُ ٱلْأَقْدَمُونَ
ayahs.translation: you and those ancient forebears of yours?

ayahs.arabic: فَإِنَّهُمْ عَدُوٌّۭ لِّىٓ إِلَّا رَبَّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: “Now [as for me, I know that,] verily, these [false deities] are my enemies, [and that none is my helper] save the Sustainer of all the worlds,

ayahs.arabic: ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَنِى فَهُوَ يَهْدِينِ
ayahs.translation: who has created me and is the One who guides me,

ayahs.arabic: وَٱلَّذِى هُوَ يُطْعِمُنِى وَيَسْقِينِ
ayahs.translation: and is the One who gives me to eat and to drink,

ayahs.arabic: وَإِذَا مَرِضْتُ فَهُوَ يَشْفِينِ
ayahs.translation: and when I fall ill, is the One who restores me to health,

ayahs.arabic: وَٱلَّذِى يُمِيتُنِى ثُمَّ يُحْيِينِ
ayahs.translation: and who will cause me to die and then will bring me back to life –

ayahs.arabic: وَٱلَّذِىٓ أَطْمَعُ أَن يَغْفِرَ لِى خَطِيٓـَٔتِى يَوْمَ ٱلدِّينِ
ayahs.translation: and who, [hope, will forgive me my faults on Judgment Day!

ayahs.arabic: رَبِّ هَبْ لِى حُكْمًۭا وَأَلْحِقْنِى بِٱلصَّٰلِحِينَ
ayahs.translation: “O my Sustainer! Endow me with the ability to judge [between right and wrong], and make me one with the righteous,

ayahs.arabic: وَٱجْعَل لِّى لِسَانَ صِدْقٍۢ فِى ٱلْءَاخِرِينَ
ayahs.translation: and grant me the power to convey the truth unto those who will come after me,

ayahs.arabic: وَٱجْعَلْنِى مِن وَرَثَةِ جَنَّةِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ
ayahs.translation: and place me among those who shall inherit the garden of bliss!

ayahs.arabic: وَٱغْفِرْ لِأَبِىٓ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ مِنَ ٱلضَّآلِّينَ
ayahs.translation: “And forgive my father - for, verily, he is among those who have gone astray –

ayahs.arabic: وَلَا تُخْزِنِى يَوْمَ يُبْعَثُونَ
ayahs.translation: and do not put me to shame on the Day when all shall be raised from the dead:

ayahs.arabic: يَوْمَ لَا يَنفَعُ مَالٌۭ وَلَا بَنُونَ
ayahs.translation: the Day on which neither wealth will be of any use, nor children,

ayahs.arabic: إِلَّا مَنْ أَتَى ٱللَّهَ بِقَلْبٍۢ سَلِيمٍۢ
ayahs.translation: [and when] only he [will be happy] who comes before God with a heart free of evil!”

ayahs.arabic: وَأُزْلِفَتِ ٱلْجَنَّةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ
ayahs.translation: For, [on that Day,] paradise will be brought within sight of the God-conscious,

ayahs.arabic: وَبُرِّزَتِ ٱلْجَحِيمُ لِلْغَاوِينَ
ayahs.translation: whereas the blazing fire will be laid open before those who had been lost in grievous error;

ayahs.arabic: وَقِيلَ لَهُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْبُدُونَ
ayahs.translation: and they will be asked: “Where now is all that you were wont to worship

ayahs.arabic: مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ هَلْ يَنصُرُونَكُمْ أَوْ يَنتَصِرُونَ
ayahs.translation: instead of God? Can these [things and beings] be of any help to you or to themselves?”

ayahs.arabic: فَكُبْكِبُوا۟ فِيهَا هُمْ وَٱلْغَاوُۥنَ
ayahs.translation: Thereupon they will be hurled into hell – they, as well as all [others] who had been lost in grievous error,

words.translation: অতঃপর মুখ নিচু করে ফেলা হবে তাদেরকে

ayahs.arabic: وَجُنُودُ إِبْلِيسَ أَجْمَعُونَ
ayahs.translation: and the hosts of Iblis - all together.

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ وَهُمْ فِيهَا يَخْتَصِمُونَ
ayahs.translation: And there and then, blaming one another, they [who had grievously sinned in life] will exclaim:

ayahs.arabic: تَٱللَّهِ إِن كُنَّا لَفِى ضَلَٰلٍۢ مُّبِينٍ
ayahs.translation: “By God, we were most obviously astray

ayahs.arabic: إِذْ نُسَوِّيكُم بِرَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: when we deemed you [false deities] equal to the Sustainer of all the worlds –

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَضَلَّنَآ إِلَّا ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ
ayahs.translation: yet they who have seduced us [into believing in you] are the truly guilty ones!

ayahs.arabic: فَمَا لَنَا مِن شَٰفِعِينَ
ayahs.translation: And now we have none to intercede for us,

ayahs.arabic: وَلَا صَدِيقٍ حَمِيمٍۢ
ayahs.translation: nor any loving friend.

ayahs.arabic: فَلَوْ أَنَّ لَنَا كَرَّةًۭ فَنَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: Would that we had a second chance [in life], so that we could be among the believers!”

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: In all this, behold, there is a message [unto men], even though most of them will not believe [in it].

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
ayahs.translation: But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!

ayahs.arabic: كَذَّبَتْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ
ayahs.translation: The people of Noah [too] gave the lie to [one of God’s] message-bearers

ayahs.arabic: إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ نُوحٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.translation: when their brother Noah said unto them: “Will you not be conscious of God?

ayahs.arabic: إِنِّى لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌۭ
ayahs.translation: Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:

ayahs.arabic: فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ
ayahs.translation: be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me!

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: “And no reward whatever do I ask of you for it: my reward rests with none but the Sustainer of all the worlds.

ayahs.arabic: فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ
ayahs.translation: Hence, remain conscious of God, and pay heed unto me!”

ayahs.arabic: ۞ قَالُوٓا۟ أَنُؤْمِنُ لَكَ وَٱتَّبَعَكَ ٱلْأَرْذَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: They answered: “Shall we place our faith in thee, even though [only] the most abject [of people] follow thee?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ وَمَا عِلْمِى بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: Said he: “And what knowledge could I have as to what they were doing [before they came to me]?

ayahs.arabic: إِنْ حِسَابُهُمْ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّى ۖ لَوْ تَشْعُرُونَ
ayahs.translation: Their reckoning rests with none but my Sus­tainer: if you could but understand [this]!

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَنَا۠ بِطَارِدِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: Hence, I shall not drive away [any of] those [who profess to be] believers;

ayahs.arabic: إِنْ أَنَا۠ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌۭ مُّبِينٌۭ
ayahs.translation: I am nothing but a plain warner.”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ لَئِن لَّمْ تَنتَهِ يَٰنُوحُ لَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمَرْجُومِينَ
ayahs.translation: Said they: “Indeed, if thou desist not, O Noah, thou wilt surely be stoned to death!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ رَبِّ إِنَّ قَوْمِى كَذَّبُونِ
ayahs.translation: [Whereupon] he prayed: “O my Sustainer! Behold, my people have given me the lie:

ayahs.arabic: فَٱفْتَحْ بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَهُمْ فَتْحًۭا وَنَجِّنِى وَمَن مَّعِىَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: hence, lay Thou wide open the truth between me and them, and save me and those of the believers who are with me!”

ayahs.arabic: فَأَنجَيْنَٰهُ وَمَن مَّعَهُۥ فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ ٱلْمَشْحُونِ
ayahs.translation: And so We saved him and those [who were] with him in the fully-laden ark,

ayahs.arabic: ثُمَّ أَغْرَقْنَا بَعْدُ ٱلْبَاقِينَ
ayahs.translation: and then We caused those who stayed behind to drown.

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: In this [story], behold, there is a message [unto men], even though most of them will not believe [in it].

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
ayahs.translation: But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace

ayahs.arabic: كَذَّبَتْ عَادٌ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ
ayahs.translation: [AND the tribe of] Ad gave the lie to [one of God’s] message-bearers

ayahs.arabic: إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ هُودٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.translation: when their brother Hud said unto them: “Will you not be conscious of God?

ayahs.arabic: إِنِّى لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌۭ
ayahs.translation: Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:

ayahs.arabic: فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ
ayahs.translation: be, then, con­scious of God, and pay heed unto me!

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: “And no reward whatever do I ask of you for it: my reward rests with none but the Sustainer of all the worlds.

ayahs.arabic: أَتَبْنُونَ بِكُلِّ رِيعٍ ءَايَةًۭ تَعْبَثُونَ
ayahs.translation: “Will you, in your wanton folly, build [idola­trous] altars on every height,

ayahs.arabic: وَتَتَّخِذُونَ مَصَانِعَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَخْلُدُونَ
ayahs.translation: and make for yourselves mighty castles, [hoping] that you might become immortal?

ayahs.arabic: وَإِذَا بَطَشْتُم بَطَشْتُمْ جَبَّارِينَ
ayahs.translation: And will you [always], whenever you lay hand [on others], lay hand [on them] cruelly, without any restraint?

ayahs.arabic: فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ
ayahs.translation: “Be, then, conscious of God and pay heed unto me:

ayahs.arabic: وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَمَدَّكُم بِمَا تَعْلَمُونَ
ayahs.translation: and [thus] be conscious of Him who has [so] amply provided you with all [the good] that you might think of

ayahs.arabic: أَمَدَّكُم بِأَنْعَٰمٍۢ وَبَنِينَ
ayahs.translation: amply provided you with flocks, and children,

ayahs.arabic: وَجَنَّٰتٍۢ وَعُيُونٍ
ayahs.translation: and gardens, and springs -:

ayahs.arabic: إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍۢ
ayahs.translation: for, verily, I fear lest suffering befall you on an awesome day!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ سَوَآءٌ عَلَيْنَآ أَوَعَظْتَ أَمْ لَمْ تَكُن مِّنَ ٱلْوَٰعِظِينَ
ayahs.translation: [But] they answered. “It is all one to us whether thou preachest [something new] or art not of those who [like to] preach.

ayahs.arabic: إِنْ هَٰذَآ إِلَّا خُلُقُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
ayahs.translation: This [religion of ours] is none other than that to which our forebears clung,

ayahs.arabic: وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ
ayahs.translation: and we are not going to be chastised [for adhering to it]!”

ayahs.arabic: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَهْلَكْنَٰهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: And so they gave him the lie: and thereupon We destroyed them. In this [story], behold, there is a message [unto men], even though most of them will not believe [in it].

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
ayahs.translation: But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!

ayahs.arabic: كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ
ayahs.translation: [AND the tribe of] Thamud gave the lie to [one of God’s] message-hearers

ayahs.arabic: إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ صَٰلِحٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.translation: when their brother Salih said unto them: “Will you not be conscious of God?

ayahs.arabic: إِنِّى لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌۭ
ayahs.translation: Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:

ayahs.arabic: فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ
ayahs.translation: be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me!

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: “And no reward whatever do I ask of you for it: my reward rests with none but the Sustainer of all the worlds.

ayahs.arabic: أَتُتْرَكُونَ فِى مَا هَٰهُنَآ ءَامِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: “Do you think that you will be left secure [forever] in the midst of what you have here and now?

ayahs.arabic: فِى جَنَّٰتٍۢ وَعُيُونٍۢ
ayahs.translation: amidst [these] gardens and springs

ayahs.arabic: وَزُرُوعٍۢ وَنَخْلٍۢ طَلْعُهَا هَضِيمٌۭ
ayahs.translation: and fields, and [these] palm-trees with slender spathes? –

ayahs.arabic: وَتَنْحِتُونَ مِنَ ٱلْجِبَالِ بُيُوتًۭا فَٰرِهِينَ
ayahs.translation: and that you will [always be able to] hew dwellings out of the mountains with [the same] great skill?”

words.translation: এবং তোমরা খোদাই করে নির্মান করেছো

ayahs.arabic: فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ
ayahs.translation: “Be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me,

ayahs.arabic: وَلَا تُطِيعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَ ٱلْمُسْرِفِينَ
ayahs.translation: and pay no heed to the counsel of those who are given to excesses –

ayahs.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا يُصْلِحُونَ
ayahs.translation: those who spread corruption on earth instead of setting things to rights!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَآ أَنتَ مِنَ ٱلْمُسَحَّرِينَ
ayahs.translation: Said they: “Thou art but one of the be­witched!

ayahs.arabic: مَآ أَنتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٌۭ مِّثْلُنَا فَأْتِ بِـَٔايَةٍ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّٰدِقِينَ
ayahs.translation: Thou art nothing but a mortal like our­selves! Come, then, forward with a token [of thy mission] if thou art a man of truth!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ هَٰذِهِۦ نَاقَةٌۭ لَّهَا شِرْبٌۭ وَلَكُمْ شِرْبُ يَوْمٍۢ مَّعْلُومٍۢ
ayahs.translation: Replied he: “This she-camel shall have a share of water, and you shall have a share of water, on the days appointed [therefor];

ayahs.arabic: وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍۢ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابُ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍۢ
ayahs.translation: and do her no harm, lest suffering befall you on an awesome day!”

ayahs.arabic: فَعَقَرُوهَا فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ نَٰدِمِينَ
ayahs.translation: But they cruelly slaughtered her - and then they had cause to regret it:

words.translation: কিন্তু তার পায়ের রগ কেটে দিয়ে হত্যা করলো

ayahs.arabic: فَأَخَذَهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: for the suffering [predicted by Salih,] befell them [then and there]. In this [story], behold, there is a message [unto men], even though most of them will not believe [in it].

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
ayahs.translation: But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone – is almighty, a dispenser of grace!

ayahs.arabic: كَذَّبَتْ قَوْمُ لُوطٍ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ
ayahs.translation: [AND] the people of Lot gave the lie to [one of God’s] message-bearers

ayahs.arabic: إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ لُوطٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.translation: when their brother Lot said unto them: “Will you not be conscious of God?

ayahs.arabic: إِنِّى لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌۭ
ayahs.translation: Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:

ayahs.arabic: فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ
ayahs.translation: be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me!

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: “And no reward whatever do I ask of you for it: my reward rests with none but the Sustainer of all the worlds.

ayahs.arabic: أَتَأْتُونَ ٱلذُّكْرَانَ مِنَ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: “Must you, of all people, [lustfully] approach men,

ayahs.arabic: وَتَذَرُونَ مَا خَلَقَ لَكُمْ رَبُّكُم مِّنْ أَزْوَٰجِكُم ۚ بَلْ أَنتُمْ قَوْمٌ عَادُونَ
ayahs.translation: keeping yourselves aloof from all the [lawful] spouses whom your Sustainer has created for you? Nay, but you are people who transgress all bounds of what is right!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ لَئِن لَّمْ تَنتَهِ يَٰلُوطُ لَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُخْرَجِينَ
ayahs.translation: Said they: “Indeed, If thou desist not, O Lot, thou wilt most certainly be expelled [from this town­ship]!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ إِنِّى لِعَمَلِكُم مِّنَ ٱلْقَالِينَ
ayahs.translation: [Lot] exclaimed: “Behold, I am one of those who utterly abhor your doings!”

ayahs.arabic: رَبِّ نَجِّنِى وَأَهْلِى مِمَّا يَعْمَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: [And then he prayed:] “O my Sustainer! Save me and my household from all that they are doing!”

ayahs.arabic: فَنَجَّيْنَٰهُ وَأَهْلَهُۥٓ أَجْمَعِينَ
ayahs.translation: Thereupon We saved him and all his household –

ayahs.arabic: إِلَّا عَجُوزًۭا فِى ٱلْغَٰبِرِينَ
ayahs.translation: all but an old woman, who was among those that stayed behind;

ayahs.arabic: ثُمَّ دَمَّرْنَا ٱلْءَاخَرِينَ
ayahs.translation: and then We utterly destroyed the others,

ayahs.arabic: وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهِم مَّطَرًۭا ۖ فَسَآءَ مَطَرُ ٱلْمُنذَرِينَ
ayahs.translation: and rained down upon them a rain [of destruction]: and dire is such rain upon all who let themselves be warned [to no avail]!

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: In this [story], behold, there is a message [unto men], even though most of them will not be­lieve [in it].

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
ayahs.translation: But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!

ayahs.arabic: كَذَّبَ أَصْحَٰبُ لْـَٔيْكَةِ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ
ayahs.translation: [AND] the dwellers of the wooded dales [of Madyan] gave the lie to [one of God’s] message-bearers

ayahs.arabic: إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ شُعَيْبٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.translation: when their brother Shu’ayb said unto them: “Will you not be conscious of God?

ayahs.arabic: إِنِّى لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌۭ
ayahs.translation: Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:

ayahs.arabic: فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ
ayahs.translation: be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me!

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: “And no reward whatever do I ask of you for it: my reward rests with none but the Sustainer of all the worlds.

ayahs.arabic: ۞ أَوْفُوا۟ ٱلْكَيْلَ وَلَا تَكُونُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْمُخْسِرِينَ
ayahs.translation: “[Always] give full measure, and be not among those who [unjustly] cause loss [to others];

ayahs.arabic: وَزِنُوا۟ بِٱلْقِسْطَاسِ ٱلْمُسْتَقِيمِ
ayahs.translation: and [in all your dealings] weigh with a true balance,

ayahs.arabic: وَلَا تَبْخَسُوا۟ ٱلنَّاسَ أَشْيَآءَهُمْ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ
ayahs.translation: and do not deprive people of what is rightfully theirs; and do not act wickedly on earth by spreading corruption,

ayahs.arabic: وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُمْ وَٱلْجِبِلَّةَ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
ayahs.translation: but be conscious of Him who has created you, just as [He created] those countless generations of old!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَآ أَنتَ مِنَ ٱلْمُسَحَّرِينَ
ayahs.translation: Said they: “Thou art but one of the be­witched,

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَنتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٌۭ مِّثْلُنَا وَإِن نَّظُنُّكَ لَمِنَ ٱلْكَٰذِبِينَ
ayahs.translation: for thou art nothing but a mortal like ourselves! And, behold, we think that thou art a consummate liar!

ayahs.arabic: فَأَسْقِطْ عَلَيْنَا كِسَفًۭا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّٰدِقِينَ
ayahs.translation: Cause, then, fragments of the sky to fall down upon us, if thou art a man of truth!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ رَبِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: Answered [Shu’ayb]: “My Sustainer knows fully well what you are doing.”

ayahs.arabic: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَخَذَهُمْ عَذَابُ يَوْمِ ٱلظُّلَّةِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ
ayahs.translation: But they gave him the lie. And thereupon suffering overtook them on a day dark with shadows: and, verily, it was the suffering of an awesome day!

words.translation: অতঃপর তারা তাকে মিথ্যা সাব্যস্ত করলো

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: In this [story], behold, there is a message [unto men], even though most of them will not believe [in it].

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
ayahs.translation: But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنَّهُۥ لَتَنزِيلُ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: Now, behold, this [divine writ] has indeed been bestowed from on high by the Sustainer of all the worlds:

ayahs.arabic: نَزَلَ بِهِ ٱلرُّوحُ ٱلْأَمِينُ
ayahs.translation: trustworthy divine inspiration has alighted with it from on high

ayahs.arabic: عَلَىٰ قَلْبِكَ لِتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُنذِرِينَ
ayahs.translation: upon thy heart, [O Muhammad] so that thou mayest be among those who preach

ayahs.arabic: بِلِسَانٍ عَرَبِىٍّۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
ayahs.translation: in the clear Arabic tongue.

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنَّهُۥ لَفِى زُبُرِ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
ayahs.translation: And, verily, [the essence of] this [revelation] is indeed found in the ancient books of divine wisdom [as well].

ayahs.arabic: أَوَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُمْ ءَايَةً أَن يَعْلَمَهُۥ عُلَمَٰٓؤُا۟ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ
ayahs.translation: Is it not evidence enough for them that [so many] learned men from among the children of Israel have recognized this [as true]?

ayahs.arabic: وَلَوْ نَزَّلْنَٰهُ عَلَىٰ بَعْضِ ٱلْأَعْجَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: But [even] had We bestowed it from on high upon any of the non-Arabs,

ayahs.arabic: فَقَرَأَهُۥ عَلَيْهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ مُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: and had he recited it unto them [in his own tongue], they would not have believed in it.

ayahs.arabic: كَذَٰلِكَ سَلَكْنَٰهُ فِى قُلُوبِ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ
ayahs.translation: Thus have We caused this [message] to pass [unheeded] through the hearts of those who are lost in sin:

ayahs.arabic: لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِۦ حَتَّىٰ يَرَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلْأَلِيمَ
ayahs.translation: they will not believe in it till they behold the grievous suffering

ayahs.arabic: فَيَأْتِيَهُم بَغْتَةًۭ وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
ayahs.translation: that will come upon them [on resurrection,] all of a sudden, without their being aware [of its approach];

ayahs.arabic: فَيَقُولُوا۟ هَلْ نَحْنُ مُنظَرُونَ
ayahs.translation: and then they will exclaim, “Could we have a respite?”

ayahs.arabic: أَفَبِعَذَابِنَا يَسْتَعْجِلُونَ
ayahs.translation: Do they, then, [really] wish that Our chas­tisement be hastened on?

ayahs.arabic: أَفَرَءَيْتَ إِن مَّتَّعْنَٰهُمْ سِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: But hast thou ever considered [this]: If We do allow them to enjoy [this life] for some years,

ayahs.arabic: ثُمَّ جَآءَهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يُوعَدُونَ
ayahs.translation: and thereupon that [chastisement] which they were promised befalls them –

ayahs.arabic: مَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يُمَتَّعُونَ
ayahs.translation: of what avail to them will be all their past enjoyments?

ayahs.arabic: وَمَآ أَهْلَكْنَا مِن قَرْيَةٍ إِلَّا لَهَا مُنذِرُونَ
ayahs.translation: And withal, never have We destroyed any community unless it had been warned

ayahs.arabic: ذِكْرَىٰ وَمَا كُنَّا ظَٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.translation: and reminded: for, never do We wrong [anyone].

ayahs.arabic: وَمَا تَنَزَّلَتْ بِهِ ٱلشَّيَٰطِينُ
ayahs.translation: And [this divine writ is such a reminder:] no evil spirits have brought it down:

ayahs.arabic: وَمَا يَنۢبَغِى لَهُمْ وَمَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ
ayahs.translation: for, neither does it suit their ends, nor is it in their power [to impart it to man]:

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّهُمْ عَنِ ٱلسَّمْعِ لَمَعْزُولُونَ
ayahs.translation: verily, [even] from hearing it are they utterly debarred!

ayahs.arabic: فَلَا تَدْعُ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا ءَاخَرَ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُعَذَّبِينَ
ayahs.translation: Hence, [O man,] do not invoke any other deity side by side with God, lest thou find thyself among those who are made to suffer [on Judgment Day].

ayahs.arabic: وَأَنذِرْ عَشِيرَتَكَ ٱلْأَقْرَبِينَ
ayahs.translation: And warn [whomever thou canst reach, beginning with] thy kinsfolk,

ayahs.arabic: وَٱخْفِضْ جَنَاحَكَ لِمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: and spread the wings of thy tenderness over all of the believers who may follow thee;

ayahs.arabic: فَإِنْ عَصَوْكَ فَقُلْ إِنِّى بَرِىٓءٌۭ مِّمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: but if they disobey thee, say, “I am free of responsibility for aught that you may do!” –

ayahs.arabic: وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ
ayahs.translation: and place thy trust in the Almighty, the Dispenser of Grace,

ayahs.arabic: ٱلَّذِى يَرَىٰكَ حِينَ تَقُومُ
ayahs.translation: who sees thee when thou standest [alone],

ayahs.arabic: وَتَقَلُّبَكَ فِى ٱلسَّٰجِدِينَ
ayahs.translation: and [sees] thy behaviour among those who prostrate themselves [before Him]:

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ
ayahs.translation: for, verily, He alone is all-hearing, all-knowing!

ayahs.arabic: هَلْ أُنَبِّئُكُمْ عَلَىٰ مَن تَنَزَّلُ ٱلشَّيَٰطِينُ
ayahs.translation: [And] shall I tell you upon whom it is that those evil spirits descend?

ayahs.arabic: تَنَزَّلُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ أَفَّاكٍ أَثِيمٍۢ
ayahs.translation: They descend upon all sinful self-deceivers

ayahs.arabic: يُلْقُونَ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ كَٰذِبُونَ
ayahs.translation: who readily lend ear [to every falsehood], and most of whom lie to others as well.

ayahs.arabic: وَٱلشُّعَرَآءُ يَتَّبِعُهُمُ ٱلْغَاوُۥنَ
ayahs.translation: And as for the poets [they, too, are prone to deceive themselves: and so, only] those who are lost in grievous error would follow them.

ayahs.arabic: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّهُمْ فِى كُلِّ وَادٍۢ يَهِيمُونَ
ayahs.translation: Art thou not aware that they roam confusedly through all the valleys [of words and thoughts],

ayahs.arabic: وَأَنَّهُمْ يَقُولُونَ مَا لَا يَفْعَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: and that they [so often] say what they do not do [or feel]?

ayahs.arabic: إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ وَذَكَرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ كَثِيرًۭا وَٱنتَصَرُوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا ظُلِمُوا۟ ۗ وَسَيَعْلَمُ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَىَّ مُنقَلَبٍۢ يَنقَلِبُونَ
ayahs.translation: [Most of them are of this kind] save those who have attained to faith, and do righteous deeds, and remember God unceasingly, and defend them­selves [only] after having been wronged, and [trust in God’s promise that] those who are bent on wrong­doing will in time come to know how evil a turn their destinies are bound to take!

ayahs.arabic: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ طسٓ ۚ تِلْكَ ءَايَٰتُ ٱلْقُرْءَانِ وَكِتَابٍۢ مُّبِينٍ
ayahs.translation: Ta. Sin. THESE ARE MESSAGES of the Qur’an - a di­vine writ clear in itself and clearly showing the truth:

ayahs.arabic: هُدًۭى وَبُشْرَىٰ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: a guidance and a glad tiding to the believers

ayahs.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَيُؤْتُونَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَهُم بِٱلْءَاخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوقِنُونَ
ayahs.translation: who are constant in prayer and spend in charity: for it is they, they who in their innermost are certain of the life to come!

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْءَاخِرَةِ زَيَّنَّا لَهُمْ أَعْمَٰلَهُمْ فَهُمْ يَعْمَهُونَ
ayahs.translation: As for those who will not believe in the life to come - behold, goodly have We made their own doings appear unto them, and so they stumble blindly to and fro

ayahs.arabic: أُو۟لَٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَهُمْ سُوٓءُ ٱلْعَذَابِ وَهُمْ فِى ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ هُمُ ٱلْأَخْسَرُونَ
ayahs.translation: It is they whom the worst of suffering awaits: for it is they, they who in the life to come shall be the greatest losers!

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنَّكَ لَتُلَقَّى ٱلْقُرْءَانَ مِن لَّدُنْ حَكِيمٍ عَلِيمٍ
ayahs.translation: But [as for thee, O believer,] verily, thou hast received this Qur’an out of the grace of One who is wise, all-knowing.

ayahs.arabic: إِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِأَهْلِهِۦٓ إِنِّىٓ ءَانَسْتُ نَارًۭا سَـَٔاتِيكُم مِّنْهَا بِخَبَرٍ أَوْ ءَاتِيكُم بِشِهَابٍۢ قَبَسٍۢ لَّعَلَّكُمْ تَصْطَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: Lo! [While lost in the desert,] Moses said to his family: “Behold, I perceive a fire [far away]; I may bring you from there some tiding [as to which way we arc to pursue], or bring you [at least] a burning brand so that you might warm yourselves.”

ayahs.arabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَهَا نُودِىَ أَنۢ بُورِكَ مَن فِى ٱلنَّارِ وَمَنْ حَوْلَهَا وَسُبْحَٰنَ ٱللَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: But when he came close to it, a call was sounded: “Blessed are all who are within [reach of] this fire, and all who are near it! And limitless in His glory is God, the Sustainer of all the worlds!”

ayahs.arabic: يَٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَنَا ٱللَّهُ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ
ayahs.translation: [And God spoke thus:] “O Moses! Verily I alone am God, the Almighty, the Wise!”

ayahs.arabic: وَأَلْقِ عَصَاكَ ۚ فَلَمَّا رَءَاهَا تَهْتَزُّ كَأَنَّهَا جَآنٌّۭ وَلَّىٰ مُدْبِرًۭا وَلَمْ يُعَقِّبْ ۚ يَٰمُوسَىٰ لَا تَخَفْ إِنِّى لَا يَخَافُ لَدَىَّ ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: [And then He said:] “Now throw down thy staff!” But when he saw it move rapidly, as if it were a serpent, he drew back [in terror], and did not [dare to] return. [And God spoke to him again:] O Moses! Fear not - for, behold, no fear need the message-bearers have in My Presence,

ayahs.arabic: إِلَّا مَن ظَلَمَ ثُمَّ بَدَّلَ حُسْنًۢا بَعْدَ سُوٓءٍۢ فَإِنِّى غَفُورٌۭ رَّحِيمٌۭ
ayahs.translation: and neither need anyone who has done wrong and then has replaced the wrong with good: for, verily, I am much-forgiving, a dis­penser of grace!

ayahs.arabic: وَأَدْخِلْ يَدَكَ فِى جَيْبِكَ تَخْرُجْ بَيْضَآءَ مِنْ غَيْرِ سُوٓءٍۢ ۖ فِى تِسْعِ ءَايَٰتٍ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَقَوْمِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًۭا فَٰسِقِينَ
ayahs.translation: “Now place thy hand into thy bosom: it will come forth [shining] white, without blemish!” [And thou shalt go] with nine [of My] messages unto Pharaoh and his people for, verily, they are people depraved!”

ayahs.arabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَتْهُمْ ءَايَٰتُنَا مُبْصِرَةًۭ قَالُوا۟ هَٰذَا سِحْرٌۭ مُّبِينٌۭ
ayahs.translation: But when Our light-giving messages came unto them, they said, “This is clearly [but] spellbinding deception!”

ayahs.arabic: وَجَحَدُوا۟ بِهَا وَٱسْتَيْقَنَتْهَآ أَنفُسُهُمْ ظُلْمًۭا وَعُلُوًّۭا ۚ فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ
ayahs.translation: - and in their wickedness and self-exaltation they rejected them, although their minds were convinced of their truth: and behold what hap­pened in the end to those spreaders of corruption!

ayahs.arabic: وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا دَاوُۥدَ وَسُلَيْمَٰنَ عِلْمًۭا ۖ وَقَالَا ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى فَضَّلَنَا عَلَىٰ كَثِيرٍۢ مِّنْ عِبَادِهِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
ayahs.translation: AND, INDEED, We granted [true] knowledge unto David and Solomon [as well]; and both were wont to say: All praise is due to God, who has [thus] favoured us above many of His believing servants!”

ayahs.arabic: وَوَرِثَ سُلَيْمَٰنُ دَاوُۥدَ ۖ وَقَالَ يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ عُلِّمْنَا مَنطِقَ ٱلطَّيْرِ وَأُوتِينَا مِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ ۖ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَهُوَ ٱلْفَضْلُ ٱلْمُبِينُ
ayahs.translation: And [in this insight] Solomon was [truly] David’s heir; and he would say: “O you people! We have been taught the speech of birds, and have been given [in abundance] of all [good] things: this, behold, is indeed a manifest favour [from God]!”

ayahs.arabic: وَحُشِرَ لِسُلَيْمَٰنَ جُنُودُهُۥ مِنَ ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلْإِنسِ وَٱلطَّيْرِ فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ
ayahs.translation: And [one day] there were assembled before Solomon his hosts of invisible beings, and of men, and of birds; and then they were led forth in orderly ranks,

ayahs.arabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَآ أَتَوْا۟ عَلَىٰ وَادِ ٱلنَّمْلِ قَالَتْ نَمْلَةٌۭ يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّمْلُ ٱدْخُلُوا۟ مَسَٰكِنَكُمْ لَا يَحْطِمَنَّكُمْ سُلَيْمَٰنُ وَجُنُودُهُۥ وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
ayahs.translation: till, when they came upon a valley [full] of ants, an ant exclaimed: “O you ants! Get into your dwellings, lest Solomon and his hosts crush you without [even] being aware [of you]!”

ayahs.arabic: فَتَبَسَّمَ ضَاحِكًۭا مِّن قَوْلِهَا وَقَالَ رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِىٓ أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَىَّ وَعَلَىٰ وَٰلِدَىَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَٰلِحًۭا تَرْضَىٰهُ وَأَدْخِلْنِى بِرَحْمَتِكَ فِى عِبَادِكَ ٱلصَّٰلِحِينَ
ayahs.translation: Thereupon [Solomon] smiled joyously at her words, and said: “O my Sustainer! Inspire me so that I may forever be grateful for those blessings of Thine with which Thou hast graced me and my parents, and that I may do what is right [in a manner] that will please Thee; and include me, by Thy grace, among Thy righteous servants!”

ayahs.arabic: وَتَفَقَّدَ ٱلطَّيْرَ فَقَالَ مَا لِىَ لَآ أَرَى ٱلْهُدْهُدَ أَمْ كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْغَآئِبِينَ
ayahs.translation: And [one day] he looked in vain for [a parti­cular one of] the birds; and so he said: How is it that I do not see the hoopoe? Or could he be among the absent?

ayahs.arabic: لَأُعَذِّبَنَّهُۥ عَذَابًۭا شَدِيدًا أَوْ لَأَا۟ذْبَحَنَّهُۥٓ أَوْ لَيَأْتِيَنِّى بِسُلْطَٰنٍۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
ayahs.translation: [If so,] I will punish him most severely or will kill him unless he bring me a convincing excuse!”

ayahs.arabic: فَمَكَثَ غَيْرَ بَعِيدٍۢ فَقَالَ أَحَطتُ بِمَا لَمْ تُحِطْ بِهِۦ وَجِئْتُكَ مِن سَبَإٍۭ بِنَبَإٍۢ يَقِينٍ
ayahs.translation: But [the hoopoe] tarried but a short while; and [when it came] it said: “I have encompassed [with my knowledge] something that thou hast never yet encompassed [with thine] - for I have come to thee from Sheba with a tiding sure!

ayahs.arabic: إِنِّى وَجَدتُّ ٱمْرَأَةًۭ تَمْلِكُهُمْ وَأُوتِيَتْ مِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ وَلَهَا عَرْشٌ عَظِيمٌۭ
ayahs.translation: “Behold, I found there a woman ruling over them; and she has been given [abundance] of all [good] things, and hers is a mighty throne,

ayahs.arabic: وَجَدتُّهَا وَقَوْمَهَا يَسْجُدُونَ لِلشَّمْسِ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَزَيَّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَٰنُ أَعْمَٰلَهُمْ فَصَدَّهُمْ عَنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ فَهُمْ لَا يَهْتَدُونَ
ayahs.translation: And I found her and her people adoring the sun instead of God; and Satan has made these doings of theirs seem goodly to them, and [thus] has barred them from the path [of God], so that they cannot find the right way:

ayahs.arabic: أَلَّا يَسْجُدُوا۟ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى يُخْرِجُ ٱلْخَبْءَ فِى ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا تُخْفُونَ وَمَا تُعْلِنُونَ
ayahs.translation: [for they have come to believe] that they ought not to adore God [although it is He] who brings forth all that is hidden in the heavens and on earth, and knows all that you would conceal as well as all that you bring into the open:

ayahs.arabic: ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ رَبُّ ٱلْعَرْشِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ ۩
ayahs.translation: God, save whom there is no deity - the Sustainer, in awesome almightiness enthroned!”

ayahs.arabic: ۞ قَالَ سَنَنظُرُ أَصَدَقْتَ أَمْ كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلْكَٰذِبِينَ
ayahs.translation: Said [Solomon]: “We shall see whether thou hast told the truth or art one of the liars!

ayahs.arabic: ٱذْهَب بِّكِتَٰبِى هَٰذَا فَأَلْقِهْ إِلَيْهِمْ ثُمَّ تَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ فَٱنظُرْ مَاذَا يَرْجِعُونَ
ayahs.translation: Go with this my letter and convey it to them; and thereafter withdraw from them and see what [answer] they return.”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَتْ يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمَلَؤُا۟ إِنِّىٓ أُلْقِىَ إِلَىَّ كِتَٰبٌۭ كَرِيمٌ
ayahs.translation: [When the Queen had read Solomon’s letter,] she said: “O you nobles! A truly distinguished letter has been conveyed unto me.

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّهُۥ مِن سُلَيْمَٰنَ وَإِنَّهُۥ بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ
ayahs.translation: Behold, it is from Solomon, and it says, ‘In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Dispenser of Grace:

ayahs.arabic: أَلَّا تَعْلُوا۟ عَلَىَّ وَأْتُونِى مُسْلِمِينَ
ayahs.translation: [God says:] Exalt not yourselves against Me, but come unto Me in willing surrender!’”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَتْ يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمَلَؤُا۟ أَفْتُونِى فِىٓ أَمْرِى مَا كُنتُ قَاطِعَةً أَمْرًا حَتَّىٰ تَشْهَدُونِ
ayahs.translation: She added: “O you nobles! Give me your opinion on the problem with which I am now faced; I would never make a [weighty] decision unless you are present with me.”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ نَحْنُ أُو۟لُوا۟ قُوَّةٍۢ وَأُو۟لُوا۟ بَأْسٍۢ شَدِيدٍۢ وَٱلْأَمْرُ إِلَيْكِ فَٱنظُرِى مَاذَا تَأْمُرِينَ
ayahs.translation: They answered: “We are endowed with power and with mighty prowess in war - but the command is thine; consider, then, what thou wouldst command.”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَتْ إِنَّ ٱلْمُلُوكَ إِذَا دَخَلُوا۟ قَرْيَةً أَفْسَدُوهَا وَجَعَلُوٓا۟ أَعِزَّةَ أَهْلِهَآ أَذِلَّةًۭ ۖ وَكَذَٰلِكَ يَفْعَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: Said she: “Verily, whenever kings enter a country they corrupt it, and turn the noblest of its people into the most abject. And this is the way they [always] behave?

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنِّى مُرْسِلَةٌ إِلَيْهِم بِهَدِيَّةٍۢ فَنَاظِرَةٌۢ بِمَ يَرْجِعُ ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: Hence, behold, I am going to send a gift to those [people], and await whatever [answer] the envoys bring back.”

ayahs.arabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَ سُلَيْمَٰنَ قَالَ أَتُمِدُّونَنِ بِمَالٍۢ فَمَآ ءَاتَىٰنِۦَ ٱللَّهُ خَيْرٌۭ مِّمَّآ ءَاتَىٰكُم بَلْ أَنتُم بِهَدِيَّتِكُمْ تَفْرَحُونَ
ayahs.translation: Now when [the Queen’s messenger] came unto Solomon, he said: “Do you people mean to add to my wealth? But that which God has given me is [so much] better than all that He has given you! Nay, it is [only such as] you that would rejoice in this gift of yours!

ayahs.arabic: ٱرْجِعْ إِلَيْهِمْ فَلَنَأْتِيَنَّهُم بِجُنُودٍۢ لَّا قِبَلَ لَهُم بِهَا وَلَنُخْرِجَنَّهُم مِّنْهَآ أَذِلَّةًۭ وَهُمْ صَٰغِرُونَ
ayahs.translation: “Go thou back unto them [that have sent thee]! For, [God says:] ‘We shall most certainly come upon them with forces which they will never be able to withstand, and shall most certainly cause them to be driven from that [land of theirs], despicable and humbled!’”

words.translation: অতঃপর অবশ্যই আমরা কাছে আসবোই তাদের
এবং অবশ্যই আমরা বের করবোই তাদেরকে

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ يَٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمَلَؤُا۟ أَيُّكُمْ يَأْتِينِى بِعَرْشِهَا قَبْلَ أَن يَأْتُونِى مُسْلِمِينَ
ayahs.translation: [When Solomon learned that the Queen of Sheba was coming,] he said [to his council]: “O you nobles! Which of you can bring me her throne ere she and her followers come unto me in willing surrender to God?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ عِفْرِيتٌۭ مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ أَنَا۠ ءَاتِيكَ بِهِۦ قَبْلَ أَن تَقُومَ مِن مَّقَامِكَ ۖ وَإِنِّى عَلَيْهِ لَقَوِىٌّ أَمِينٌۭ
ayahs.translation: Said a bold one of the invisible beings [subject to Solomon]: “I shall bring it to thee ere thou rise from thy council-seat - for, behold, I am powerful enough to do it, [and] worthy of trust!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ ٱلَّذِى عِندَهُۥ عِلْمٌۭ مِّنَ ٱلْكِتَٰبِ أَنَا۠ ءَاتِيكَ بِهِۦ قَبْلَ أَن يَرْتَدَّ إِلَيْكَ طَرْفُكَ ۚ فَلَمَّا رَءَاهُ مُسْتَقِرًّا عِندَهُۥ قَالَ هَٰذَا مِن فَضْلِ رَبِّى لِيَبْلُوَنِىٓ ءَأَشْكُرُ أَمْ أَكْفُرُ ۖ وَمَن شَكَرَ فَإِنَّمَا يَشْكُرُ لِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ رَبِّى غَنِىٌّۭ كَرِيمٌۭ
ayahs.translation: Answered he who was illumined by revela­tion: “[Nay,] as for me - I shall bring it to thee ere the twinkling of thy eye ceases!” And when he saw it truly before him, he exclaimed: “This is [an outcome] of my Sustainer’s bounty, to test me as to whether I am grateful or ungrateful! However, he who is grateful [to God] is but grateful for his own good; and he who is ungrate­ful [should know that], verily, my Sustainer is self-sufficient, most generous in giving!”

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ نَكِّرُوا۟ لَهَا عَرْشَهَا نَنظُرْ أَتَهْتَدِىٓ أَمْ تَكُونُ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَهْتَدُونَ
ayahs.translation: [And] he continued: Alter her throne so that she may not know it as hers: let us see whether she allows herself to be guided [to the truth] or remains one of those who will not be guided.”

ayahs.arabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَتْ قِيلَ أَهَٰكَذَا عَرْشُكِ ۖ قَالَتْ كَأَنَّهُۥ هُوَ ۚ وَأُوتِينَا ٱلْعِلْمَ مِن قَبْلِهَا وَكُنَّا مُسْلِمِينَ
ayahs.translation: And so, as soon as she arrived, she was asked: “Is thy throne like this?” She answered: “It is as though it were the same!” [And Solomon said to his nobles: “She has arrived at the truth without any help from us,] although it is we who have been given [divine] knowledge before her, and have [long ago] surrendered ourselves unto God!

ayahs.arabic: وَصَدَّهَا مَا كَانَت تَّعْبُدُ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّهَا كَانَتْ مِن قَوْمٍۢ كَٰفِرِينَ
ayahs.translation: [And she has recognized the truth] although that which she has been wont to worship instead of God had kept her away [from the right path]: for, behold, she is descended of people who deny the truth!”

words.translation: আর তাকে বিরত রেখেছিলো (ঈমান আনা হ'তে)

ayahs.arabic: قِيلَ لَهَا ٱدْخُلِى ٱلصَّرْحَ ۖ فَلَمَّا رَأَتْهُ حَسِبَتْهُ لُجَّةًۭ وَكَشَفَتْ عَن سَاقَيْهَا ۚ قَالَ إِنَّهُۥ صَرْحٌۭ مُّمَرَّدٌۭ مِّن قَوَارِيرَ ۗ قَالَتْ رَبِّ إِنِّى ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِى وَأَسْلَمْتُ مَعَ سُلَيْمَٰنَ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.translation: [After a while] she was told: “Enter this court!” - but when she saw it, she thought that it was a fathomless expanse of water, and she bared her legs. Said he: “Behold, it is [but] a court smoothly paved with glass!” Cried she: “O my Sustainer! I have been sinning against myself thy worshipping aught but Thee]: but [now] I have surrendered myself, with Solomon, unto the Sustainer of all the worlds!”

ayahs.arabic: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَٰلِحًا أَنِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ فَإِذَا هُمْ فَرِيقَانِ يَخْتَصِمُونَ
ayahs.translation: AND [likewise], indeed, We sent unto [the tribe of] Thamud their brother Salih [with this message]: “Worship God alone!” and, behold, they were [split into] two factions contending with one another.

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ يَٰقَوْمِ لِمَ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِٱلسَّيِّئَةِ قَبْلَ ٱلْحَسَنَةِ ۖ لَوْلَا تَسْتَغْفِرُونَ ٱللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ
ayahs.translation: Said [Salih to the erring ones]: Why do you seek to hasten the coming upon you of evil instead of hoping for the good?” Why do you not, rather, ask God to forgive you your sins, so that you might be graced with His mercy?”

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ ٱطَّيَّرْنَا بِكَ وَبِمَن مَّعَكَ ۚ قَالَ طَٰٓئِرُكُمْ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ بَلْ أَنتُمْ قَوْمٌۭ تُفْتَنُونَ
ayahs.translation: They answered: “We augur evil from thee and those that follow thee!” Said he: “Your destiny, good or evil, rests with God yea, you are people undergoing a test!”

ayahs.arabic: وَكَانَ فِى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ تِسْعَةُ رَهْطٍۢ يُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا يُصْلِحُونَ
ayahs.translation: Now there were in the city nine men who were wont to commit deeds of depravity all over the land, and would not reform;

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ تَقَاسَمُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ لَنُبَيِّتَنَّهُۥ وَأَهْلَهُۥ ثُمَّ لَنَقُولَنَّ لِوَلِيِّهِۦ مَا شَهِدْنَا مَهْلِكَ أَهْلِهِۦ وَإِنَّا لَصَٰدِقُونَ
ayahs.translation: [and] after having bound one another by an oath in God’s name, they said: “Indeed, we shall suddenly fall upon him and his household by night [and slay them all]; and then we shall boldly say to his next of kin, ‘We did not witness the destruction of his household - and, behold, we are indeed men of truth!’”

words.translation: অবশ্যই আমরা তাকে রাতে আক্রমণ করবোই

ayahs.arabic: وَمَكَرُوا۟ مَكْرًۭا وَمَكَرْنَا مَكْرًۭا وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
ayahs.translation: And so they devised an evil scheme; but We devised a subtle scheme (of Our own), and they perceived it not.

ayahs.arabic: فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَٰقِبَةُ مَكْرِهِمْ أَنَّا دَمَّرْنَٰهُمْ وَقَوْمَهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ
ayahs.translation: Behold, then, what all their scheming came to in the end: We utterly destroyed them and their people, all of them;

ayahs.arabic: فَتِلْكَ بُيُوتُهُمْ خَاوِيَةًۢ بِمَا ظَلَمُوٓا۟ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَةًۭ لِّقَوْمٍۢ يَعْلَمُونَ
ayahs.translation: and [now] those dwellings of theirs are empty, [ruined] as an outcome of their evil deeds. In this, behold, there is a message indeed for peo­ple of [innate] knowledge –

ayahs.arabic: وَأَنجَيْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَكَانُوا۟ يَتَّقُونَ
ayahs.translation: seeing that We saved those who had attained to faith and were conscious of Us,

ayahs.arabic: وَلُوطًا إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِۦٓ أَتَأْتُونَ ٱلْفَٰحِشَةَ وَأَنتُمْ تُبْصِرُونَ
ayahs.translation: AND [thus, too, did We save] Lot, when he said unto his people: “Would you commit this abomination with your eyes open (to its being against all nature)?

ayahs.arabic: أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ ٱلرِّجَالَ شَهْوَةًۭ مِّن دُونِ ٱلنِّسَآءِ ۚ بَلْ أَنتُمْ قَوْمٌۭ تَجْهَلُونَ
ayahs.translation: Must you really approach men with lust instead of women? Nay, but you are people without any awareness (of right and wrong)!”
